meta.title: Automated Prompt Engineering with DSPy
meta.description:
➡️ Lifetime access to ADVANCED-inference Repo (incl. DSPy scripts in this vid.): https://trelis.com/ADVANCED-inference/
➡️ Runpod Affiliate Link: https://runpod.io?ref=jmfkcdio
➡️ One-click GPU templates: https://github.com/TrelisResearch/one-click-llms

OTHER TRELIS LINKS:
➡️ Trelis Newsletter: https://blog.Trelis.com
➡️ Trelis Resources and Support: https://Trelis.com/About

VIDEO LINKS:
- Slides: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1U5nzaoTmybgFf0l6x02pYZuMLXqORlvISLjUYKn5iCg/edit?usp=sharing
- DSPy intro notebook: https://github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy/blob/71e5d35cc74ef959f5cc17dff42b7d78eb361ddd/intro.ipynb#l4l 
- DSPy assertions notebook: https://github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy/blob/main/examples/qa/hotpot/hotpotqa_with_assertions.ipynb

TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 Introduction to structured/automated prompt generation
0:41 Video Overview
2:10 Why is DSPy useful?
3:41 Q&A and Retrieval Databases (hotpotqa and wikipedia)
4:53 Notebooks - DSPy and Trelis
6:08 Installation and Setup
9:24 Step 1: Simple Prompting and Benchmarking
12:49 Step 2: Adding Chain of Thought
15:08 Step 3: Add vector search (retrieval)
19:00 Step 4: Add (random) few shot examples
24:00 Step 5: Optimally choosing few shot examples
30:30 Step 6: Add multi-hop search/queries (Baleen / Perplexity-style)
36:14 Step 7: Multi-hop search WITH optimally chosen few shot examples
40:54 Overall Results Comparison!
43:21 DSPy assertions - for further answer quality control
45:21 Video resources
